title: parse_relay_addr: IPv6 host without port parses incorrectly
description:
parse_relay_addr() in core/src/transport.rs uses rsplit_once(':') to split host from port. For an IPv6 literal in brackets with an explicit port (e.g. ws://[::1]:3210/ws), rsplit_once finds the last colon correctly and produces ([::1], 3210). That case works.

However, for an IPv6 literal WITHOUT an explicit port (e.g. ws://[::1]/ws), host_port becomes [::1]. rsplit_once(':') on [::1] finds the last colon inside the brackets and splits as ([:, 1]), then tries to parse 1] as a u16 port — which fails with a parse error. The caller gets Err instead of the correct ("[::1]", 80).

There is no unit test for ws://[::1]/ws or wss://[::1]/ws. A test would expose this parse failure. The fix requires bracket-aware IPv6 parsing.

Note: the production relay is accessed via hostname (not IPv6 literal), so this is latent rather than currently reachable, but it is a correctness gap in the documented interface.
close_reason: Fixed by IPv6 bracket handling implementation
